OFFICE OF THE
CITY MANAGER
(4ls) 877-8500
December L7, 1990
Ms. JilI R. Stecher
Counsel, Legal Oivision
Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
428 rrJrr Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Dear Ms. Stecher:
By letter of October 12, 1990 to City Attorney Valerie J.
Armento, you advised that based on the facts presented to you at
that time, Councilmember John Penna t'may not participate in
decisions regarding the Shearwater Redevelopment Project. " ( I
recognize this conclusion may change based on the set of facts
presented to you. ) The facts as recited in your letter note that
the City and the developer of the project have been involved in
negotiations regarding a potential property transaction and that
direction had been provided to the City Manager by the City
Council. In the course of discussions with the Council,
information was presented in written form to the Council by me to
enable them to discuss potential disposition of the property.
The information was not provided to Councilmember Penna.
Subsequently, Councilmember Penna has reguested copies of the
written information that was presented to the Council. On
December 13, in a telephone conversation, I inquired as to
whether Councilmember Penna may be provided this information.
Based on our conversation, it is my understanding you indicated
the Political Reform Act does not directly address this question
and that there is no specific provision that indicates Mr. Penna
is not entitled to this information. 
(Subsequent to our
conversation, I was furnished a copy of the Hamilton v. Town of
Los Gatos decision, a copy of which is attached. )
I am writing at this time to seek confirmation that my
recollection is correct and verification that there is no
regulation in the Political Reform Act that would preclude my
furnishing Councilmember Penna infornation that was presented to
4OO GBAND AVENUE _ P. O. BOX 711 _ 9163


Ms. JilI R. stecher
Council , Legal Divlsion
Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
December 17, L99O
Page 2
the Council as described above. I would appreciate an
expeditious response to this inquiry as Councilmember Penna is
desirous of an opportunity to receive this information as quickly
as possible.
Yours very truly,
\,,n

Jesris Armas

sections.question: Whether Councilmember John Penna may be provided written information that was presented to the City Council regarding a potential property transaction and the Shearwater Redevelopment Project, given that he has been advised he may not participate in decisions regarding that project.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a city manager provide a councilmember who is disqualified from participating in a matter with written information about that matter that was presented to the full council?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city manager to FPPC counsel, not an FPPC response letter. Document quality is poor with OCR errors. References a prior October 12, 1990 FPPC advice letter to City Attorney Armento. No formal Q/C sections present; question reconstructed from letter content.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Councilmember John Penna may be provided written information that was presented to the City Council regarding a potential property transaction and the Shearwater Redevelopment Project, given that he has been advised he may not participate in decisions regarding that project.

embedding.summary: The City Manager of a California city requests FPPC confirmation that the Political Reform Act does not preclude providing Councilmember Penna with written information about the Shearwater Redevelopment Project that was shared with the City Council, despite the councilmember's prior disqualification from participating in decisions regarding that project.
